title: rosa dumalis subsp. subcollina
intro: Rosa dumalis subsp. subcollina, also known as the Himalayan rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, specifically a type of wild rose native to the Himalayas. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Rosa dumalis subsp. subcollina are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating gastrointestinal issues, and acting as an antiseptic. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the form of dried flowers or as a tincture.
